The Arecaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Cephalocarpus ptariensis being a part of the tribe Areceae. The Areceae tribe is characterized by its unique palm-like characteristics and is primarily found in tropical regions. As a member of the Arecaceae family, Cephalocarpus ptariensis is closely related to other palm species.
